---
title: Jinja Syntax
---

*Calepin* uses [MiniJinja](https://github.com/mitsuhiko/minijinja), a Jinja2 template engine for Rust. The same syntax is used everywhere: in document bodies ([shortcuts](shortcuts.qmd)), in element templates ([elements](elements.qmd)), in page templates ([pages](pages.qmd)), and in site templates ([sites](sites.qmd)).

This page covers the syntax basics. If you know Jinja2 or Python's Django templates, you already know this.


# Expressions

Insert a value with double braces:

```
{% raw %}{{ variable }}{% endraw %}
```

Dot notation accesses nested values:

```
{% raw %}{{ meta.title }}
{{ var.urls.docs }}{% endraw %}
```


# Functions

Call functions with parentheses and keyword arguments:

```
{% raw %}{{ pagebreak() }}
{{ video(url="https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc") }}
{{ lipsum(paragraphs=3) }}{% endraw %}
```


# Conditionals

```
{% raw %}{% if format == "html" %}
This appears only in HTML output.
{% elif format == "latex" %}
This appears only in LaTeX output.
{% else %}
This appears in all other formats.
{% endif %}{% endraw %}
```


# Loops

```
{% raw %}{% for item in var.features %}
- {{ item }}
{% endfor %}{% endraw %}
```


# Filters

Filters transform values with the pipe operator:

```
{% raw %}{{ meta.title | upper }}
{{ meta.title | length }}{% endraw %}
```

MiniJinja provides built-in filters like `upper`, `lower`, `length`, `trim`, `replace`, `default`, and others. See the [MiniJinja documentation](https://docs.rs/minijinja/latest/minijinja/) for the full list.


# Comments

```
{% raw %}{# This is a comment and won't appear in the output. #}{% endraw %}
```


# Raw blocks

To include literal Jinja syntax without evaluation, wrap it in `{% raw %}{% raw %}...{% endraw %}{% endraw %}`:

````
{% raw %}{% raw %}
{{ this is not evaluated }}
{% endraw %}{% endraw %}
````


# Whitespace control

Add a dash to trim whitespace before or after a tag:

```
{% raw %}{%- if condition -%}
  compact output
{%- endif -%}{% endraw %}
```


# Set variables

Define local variables:

```
{% raw %}{% set name = "World" %}
Hello, {{ name }}!{% endraw %}
```


# Include files

Include external files:

```
{% raw %}{% include "header.qmd" %}{% endraw %}
```

In document bodies, `{% raw %}{% include %}{% endraw %}` is a pre-parse directive --- included content is parsed as blocks, so code chunks and fenced divs work normally. YAML front matter in included files is stripped automatically.


# Where this syntax is used

| Context | Example | Details |
|---------|---------|---------|
| Document body | `{% raw %}{{ meta.title }}{% endraw %}` in your `.qmd` | [Shortcuts](shortcuts.qmd) |
| Element templates | `{% raw %}{{children}}{% endraw %}` in `_calepin/elements/div.html` | [Elements](elements.qmd) |
| Page templates | `{% raw %}{{body}}{% endraw %}` in `_calepin/templates/calepin.html` | [Pages](pages.qmd) |
| Site templates | `{% raw %}{{ page.title }}{% endraw %}` in `_templates/page.html` | [Sites](sites.qmd) |

The syntax is identical everywhere. The only difference is which variables and functions are available in each context.
